_meta.purpose: Reference data for all real-world circuits in F1 25 (base game 2025 calendar) plus new tracks added by the 2026 Season Pack. Consumed by sim for pit-loss, SC probability, and per-track modeling flavor.

confidenceLegend.confirmed: Directly stated by an official EA/F1/FIA source.
confidenceLegend.reasonable_estimate: Derived from circuit characteristics (pit lane length/speed limit, wall proximity, historical reputation) that are individually well-documented, but not from one single published table for this exact figure.
confidenceLegend.placeholder: No usable source or estimate basis yet; a plain default the sim should not treat as calibrated.

_meta.importantCorrectionToBrief: The project brief (PitWallAI_Multi_Agent_Plan.md) lists 8 venues as 'LiDAR-scanned': Silverstone, Bahrain, Imola, Melbourne, Miami, Suzuka, Red Bull Ring, Zandvoort. Actual F1 25 sourcing does NOT support this. Only 5 venues are LiDAR-scanned in F1 25: Bahrain, Miami, Melbourne, Suzuka, Imola. Silverstone, Red Bull Ring, and Zandvoort are instead the 3 venues with REVERSE LAYOUT support — a separate, unrelated feature. These are two different confirmed-accuracy signals and should not be merged into one 'high confidence' bucket. See each track's lidarScanned and reverseLayoutAvailable fields — do not assume they overlap.

calendar2025.note: Full 24-round 2025 real-world calendar, all included in F1 25 base game per EA (every 2025 GP venue is in the game).

changes: Spanish GP moves from Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya to a new circuit, Madring (IFEMA-Madrid), Sept 11-13 2026. Hybrid street/permanent layout, 5.4km, 22 corners, similar concept to Miami (permanent-feel hybrid, not a pure street circuit). New circuitId: madring.
2026 Season Pack brings the total in-game circuit count to 25 (all 2026-calendar venues included).
Canadian GP moved to an earlier date relative to Miami for 2026 logistics; round order shifts but venue set is otherwise the same as 2025 minus Barcelona plus Madring.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Mid-length pit lane, standard 80km/h-equivalent speed limit, no unusually long entry/exit.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Historically Albert Park had frequent early-race incidents at Turn 1/3 in its older layout; the 2022+ reprofiled layout has fewer walls and lower SC frequency than its reputation suggests, so 'medium' rather than 'high'.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Long pit lane, known as one of the longer time-loss circuits.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Wide, permanent circuit with some barrier-heavy sections (Turn 14); moderate historical SC rate.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Tight, tricky pit entry (130R approach) but standard-length pit lane.

safetyCarHistory.basis: High-speed, low-barrier-contact layout in the dry; weather (typhoon season) is the main SC/red-flag driver historically, not incidents.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Long, wide pit straight; standard desert-circuit layout.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Wide run-off areas, few walls; historically one of the lower-SC-frequency circuits.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Fast street circuit, high pit-lane entry/exit speed limits offset by wall-lined narrow lane.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Very high average speed, walls close to track edge in multiple sections; has had SC/red-flag incidents most years since 2021 debut.

circuits.name: Miami International Autodrome
circuits.circuitType: permanent (built around Hard Rock Stadium, street-hybrid feel)

pitLaneLoss.basis: Relatively short, wide pit lane by modern standards.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Tight chicane sections (Turns 14-17) have produced incidents in most editions since 2022 debut.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Widely cited as one of the highest pit-loss circuits on the calendar due to a long, low-speed-limit pit lane relative to a short, fast lap.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Narrow, wall-lined old-school layout (Villeneuve/Tosa/Rivazza) with limited run-off; regular SC history including notable 2020-2022 incidents.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Short pit lane in absolute terms, but the low speed limit and tight entry keep the loss non-trivial despite the short physical length.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Tightest, most barrier-lined circuit on the calendar; historically among the most SC/red-flag-prone races.

circuits.name: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya

pitLaneLoss.basis: Standard permanent-circuit pit lane length and speed limit.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Wide run-off, low wall contact; historically one of the calendar's cleanest circuits for SC frequency.

circuits.notes: Replaced on the 2026 calendar by Madring; retained here as a 2025-relevant circuit still in F1 25's base game.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Widely regarded as one of the shortest pit-loss circuits on the calendar — short pit lane, minimal detour from the racing line.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Walls close to the track (notably the Wall of Champions at the final chicane); one of the calendar's most SC-prone circuits historically.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Short lap, standard pit lane; low-to-mid time loss.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Short, high-density-of-incidents lap (multiple slow corners feeding straights) tends to produce a moderate SC rate despite generous run-off.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Long, fast lap somewhat offsets a standard-length pit lane, giving a moderate relative loss.

safetyCarHistory.basis: High-speed corners (Copse/Maggotts-Becketts) raise incident severity when they happen, but generous run-off in most sections keeps frequency moderate rather than high.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Long lap and a pit entry that allows relatively high speed keep the relative time loss on the lower end.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Longest circuit on the calendar with famously variable Ardennes weather; frequent weather-driven SC/red-flag periods (e.g. 2021 red-flagged race, 2023-25 rain-affected sessions).

pitLaneLoss.basis: Tight, twisty permanent circuit; standard mid-pack pit loss.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Low average speed and generous run-off historically keep this one of the calendar's lower-SC circuits, despite being tight and overtaking-poor.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Narrow circuit with a tight, chicane-heavy layout increases relative pit-loss.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Narrow track with limited overtaking and dune-side barriers in places; moderate SC history since its 2021 return.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Very high average lap speed makes even a standard-length pit lane detour costly in relative terms — commonly cited as one of the higher-loss circuits despite a 'fast' reputation.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Wide run-off at most chicanes/corners keeps incident rate low; mainly a low-SC circuit historically.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Standard street-circuit pit lane, offset by a very long, fast back straight that lowers the relative cost of the lap portion.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Narrow street circuit with a notoriously tight castle-section chicane; one of the calendar's most SC/red-flag-prone venues most seasons.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Long, walled street-circuit pit lane; consistently one of the highest time-loss venues.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Historically near-100% SC probability most years (only missed once, 2023, since the race joined the calendar) — the single most SC-guaranteed circuit on the calendar.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Standard permanent-circuit pit lane.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Generous run-off in most sections; occasional Turn 1 first-lap incidents keep it from being 'low'.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Long pit lane through the stadium section area.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Wide track surface, generous run-off in most corners; historically moderate-to-low SC rate.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Short lap, standard pit lane.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Frequently wet/mixed-weather race weekend historically (Sao Paulo's rainy season overlaps the race date), driving up SC/VSC frequency independent of circuit layout risk.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Street circuit with very long, fast straights (Strip); standard street-circuit pit lane.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Only debuted in 2023; sample size of 2-3 real races is too small for a confident historical tier yet. Treat as placeholder pending more data.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Long, high-speed lap makes a standard pit lane detour relatively costly.

safetyCarHistory.basis: High-speed, sweeping layout with some wall proximity; moderate historical SC rate.

pitLaneLoss.basis: Standard permanent-circuit pit lane.

safetyCarHistory.basis: Sourced figure: ~38% Safety Car probability and ~38% VSC probability historically.

circuits.name: Madring (IFEMA - Madrid)
circuits.circuitType: hybrid street/permanent (built from real CAD data, comparable to Miami's hybrid feel)

pitLaneLoss.basis: New venue, no real-world race data yet (first race Sept 2026); no basis for even an informed estimate yet beyond 'hybrid circuit similar to Miami' as a rough analogue.

safetyCarHistory.basis: No historical races run yet at time of writing (2026-07-09, first race scheduled Sept 11-13 2026).

circuits.notes: New to the 2026 F1 calendar and the F1 25: 2026 Season Pack. 5.4km, 22 corners. Replaces Barcelona as the Spanish GP host.
